note: Generated /for/* vertical pages. Slugs MUST NOT collide with hand-authored /for pages (agents, edge-ai, fintech, government-defense, healthcare, support-automation). Regulatory references are framed as the customer's own obligations the architecture supports, not a kolm certification claim — keep clear of FORBIDDEN_PUBLIC_PATTERNS (no 'HIPAA-ready', 'EU AI Act compliant', etc.).

verticals.name: Insurance
verticals.bluf: Insurers process claims and policy documents that cannot be sent to a public LLM API on every call. kolm lets an insurer compile a claims specialist that runs inside its own boundary, with PII redacted on capture and a signed receipt for every model build.
verticals.regs_intro: Insurance carriers operate under model-governance and privacy obligations that favor inspectable, owned models over opaque external APIs. kolm's architecture supports those obligations:
verticals.kolm_flow: Run the kolm gateway in your environment, capture (claim, decision) and (policy, answer) pairs with inline redaction, distill a specialist, gate it on a calibrated K-score, and compile a quantized artifact you serve on-prem.
verticals.proof_stat_context: Inline PII detection scored F1 1.0 with zero false positives on the public redaction benchmark, so claimant data is scrubbed before any storage.
verticals.proof_quote: We needed a model-risk story our examiners could follow end to end. A signed receipt for every build is exactly that.

faqs.q: Does claimant data leave our network?
faqs.a: With a self-hosted gateway and a compiled local model, the pipeline runs inside your boundary, and PII is redacted on the capture path before storage.

faqs.q: How do we evidence a model build for examiners?
faqs.a: Each compile emits an Ed25519-signed receipt recording the input weight hashes, method, and K-score, replayable at /verify-prod.

faqs.q: Can we keep human review in the loop?
faqs.a: Yes. kolm produces a model artifact; how you route its outputs through adjuster review is your workflow decision.

verticals.name: Legal services
verticals.bluf: Law firms handle privileged documents that must not be sent to a third-party API. kolm lets a firm compile a contract or research specialist that runs inside its own boundary, with PII redacted on capture and a verifiable build receipt for every model.
verticals.regs_intro: Legal practice carries strict confidentiality and privilege duties that make an owned, inspectable model preferable to an external API. kolm's architecture supports those duties:
verticals.kolm_flow: Capture (document, extraction) and (question, memo) pairs through a self-hosted gateway with redaction, distill a specialist tuned to your matter types, and compile a quantized artifact served on firm hardware.
verticals.proof_stat_context: On the public redaction benchmark, PII detection scored F1 1.0 with zero false positives, so privileged text is scrubbed before storage.
verticals.proof_quote: Privilege means client text cannot leave. An owned model with a signed build receipt let us say yes to AI without that risk.

faqs.q: Does privileged content reach a third party?
faqs.a: With a self-hosted gateway and a compiled local model, inference stays inside the firm, and PII is redacted on capture.

faqs.q: Is the model tuned to our matters?
faqs.a: Distillation trains on your captured pairs, so the specialist reflects your matter types and drafting conventions.

faqs.q: Can we prove what was built?
faqs.a: Yes. Every compile is signed and replayable at /verify-prod, giving a clean provenance trail for the build.

verticals.name: Manufacturing
verticals.bluf: Manufacturers want AI on the plant floor and in field service without a metered cloud round-trip. kolm lets a manufacturer compile a maintenance or quality specialist that runs at the edge, with a signed build receipt and no per-token bill.
verticals.regs_intro: Manufacturing environments often isolate operational technology and demand offline reliability, which favors an owned model over a cloud API. kolm's architecture supports those constraints:
verticals.kolm_flow: Capture (symptom, fix) and (spec, answer) pairs through the gateway, distill a maintenance specialist, and compile a heavily quantized artifact that runs on edge hardware with no network.
verticals.proof_stat_context: kolm can compress a small base model to 0.44 GB INT4 in the verified matrix — small enough for constrained edge devices on the plant floor.
verticals.proof_quote: Our line cannot depend on a cloud call. An owned model that runs offline with a verifiable build is the only thing that fit.

faqs.q: Can a kolm model run with no network?
faqs.a: Yes. The compiled artifact serves locally with no inference-time network dependency, suitable for isolated OT networks.

faqs.q: How small can the model get?
faqs.a: The verified matrix records sizes from 0.44 GB up. You choose the base and quantization to fit your edge hardware.

faqs.q: What proves the deployed model is correct?
faqs.a: The signed receipt records the exact weights and method; verify it at /verify-prod or with kolm verify before deploying to the line.
